page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 96 vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 744 GB of RAM, providing approximately 7.75 GB of memory per core. It utilizes the x86_64 architecture and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier performance in multi-threaded operations and CPU-intensive tasks. The instance achieved top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and multi-core evaluations, as well as PassMark CPU and extended instruction tests, aided by advanced vector and matrix extensions including AVX-512 and AMX. Multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and static web serving throughput also rank in the top tier. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 288.74 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. Large-model LLM prompt processing performs strongly, though text generation speeds for large models fall into the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the c4-highmem-96 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that require substantial CPU compute without the overhead of dedicated GPU accelerators. However, the lack of bundled local storage means storage requirements must be met via network-attached volumes, which represents a key architectural tradeoff.

This instance is highly suited for memory-intensive and compute-heavy enterprise workloads. Appropriate use cases include large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and CPU-based machine learning inference, particularly prompt processing.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized server instance featuring 96 vCPUs and 744 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 48 physical cores. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers top-tier CPU performance, as evidenced by high Geekbench and PassMark scores, alongside strong memory bandwidth. It includes advanced hardware extensions such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated matrix operations. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, representing a tradeoff for workloads requiring direct-attached high-speed disks. It is highly efficient for memory-intensive applications, database operations, high-throughput static web serving, and CPU-based LLM prompt processing.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 is a memory-optimized instance with 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C CPU, delivering top-tier compute and memory bandwidth for database and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-96 features 96 vCPUs, 744 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, optimized for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory and compute instance featuring ninety-six virtual cores and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 vCPUs and 744 GB RAM powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated matrix and vector operations
Top-tier performance in multi-threaded decompression and CPU benchmarks
Strong memory read bandwidth of up to 288.74 GB/sec for large blocks
Optimized for large-scale databases, web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference
